engineGuide.h2: Nissan X-Trail Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Nissan X-Trail across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Nissan's workhorse 2.0 dCi, fitted to T31/T32 X-Trail; renowned for torque but timing chain stretch at high mileage.
compatibleVariants: Acenta 2.0 dCi, Acenta Premium 2.0 dCi, Columbia 2.0 dCi, N-Connecta 2.0 dCi, Tekna 2.0 dCi, Tekna+ 2.0 dCi, Visia 2.0 dCi (all 4WD options)
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch causing cam-crank correlation codes (P0011) at 80k-120k miles
Turbocharger oil line leaks leading to oil starvation
DPF clogging on short-journey vehicles requiring specialist cleaning
entries.cta: Get quotes for M9R Nissan X-Trail engine replacement

entries.title: 1.6L Diesel
entries.history: Downsized 1.6 dCi introduced with T32; improved economy but DPF and turbo sensitivity on urban use.
compatibleVariants: Acenta 1.6 dCi, Acenta Premium 1.6 dCi, N-Connecta 1.6 dCi, Tekna 1.6 dCi, Tekna+ 1.6 dCi, Visia 1.6 dCi
commonFailures: DPF regeneration failure causing limp mode and power loss
Turbocharger actuator faults at 70k-100k miles
EGR valve clogging affecting idle quality
entries.cta: Get quotes for R9M Nissan X-Trail engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Early T30 diesel unit; robust block but cam-crank synchronization issues after timing work.
compatibleVariants: Sport 2.2 dCi, SVE 2.2 dCi, Columbia 2.2 dCi

entries.title: crank synchronization failure after timing belt replacement
entries.cta: Get quotes for YD22DDTi Nissan X-Trail engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Reliable naturally aspirated petrol for early X-Trail; simple design but higher fuel consumption than diesels.
compatibleVariants: S 2.0, SE 2.0, Sport 2.0, Columbia 2.0, Acenta 2.0, Visia 2.0 (T30/T31)
commonFailures: Ignition coil failures causing misfires at 60k-90k miles
Timing chain tensioner wear on high-mileage examples
Oil consumption increase after 100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for QR20DE Nissan X-Trail engine replacement

entries.title: 2.5L Petrol
entries.history: Larger displacement option for T30/T31; stronger torque for 4WD use but thirstier than 2.0L units.
commonFailures: Oil consumption due to piston ring wear at 80k+ miles
CVT transmission compatibility issues on auto models
Coolant leak from timing cover gasket
entries.cta: Get quotes for QR25DE Nissan X-Trail engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Refined replacement for QR20DE in T31; smoother operation and improved emissions compliance.
commonFailures: Timing cover oil leak requiring £1,000-£1,500 repair
CVT transmission fluid degradation on auto variants
Camshaft position sensor faults causing rough idle
entries.cta: Get quotes for MR20DE Nissan X-Trail engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Direct Injection Petrol
entries.history: Direct injection evolution for T32 hybrid variants; improved efficiency but carbon buildup on intake valves.

entries.title: Hybrid, N-Connecta Hybrid (T32 MR20DD hybrid)
commonFailures: Carbon buildup on intake valves affecting performance

entries.title: pressure fuel pump wear at 70k+ miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for MR20DD Nissan X-Trail engine replacement

entries.title: 1.3L DIG-T Petrol
entries.history: Mercedes-sourced turbo petrol for late T32; downsized efficiency with turbo complexity.

entries.title: Connecta 1.3 DIG-T, Tekna 1.3 DIG-T (T32 facelift)
commonFailures: Turbocharger oil seal leaks causing blue smoke
Timing chain tensioner rattle on cold start
Coolant loss from thermostat housing
entries.cta: Get quotes for HR13DDT Nissan X-Trail engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L VC-Turbo e-POWER
entries.history: Variable compression turbo hybrid for T33; electric drive with petrol generator, complex but efficient.

entries.title: Connecta e-POWER, Tekna e-POWER, Tekna+ e-POWER (T33)
commonFailures: Hybrid inverter cooling system faults

entries.title: Turbo actuator calibration issues
entries.cta: Get quotes for KR15DDT Nissan X-Trail engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Nissan X-Trail Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Nissan X-Trail's M9R, R9M and QR/MR engine families dominate UK replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your X-Trail.

problems.group: Nissan X-Trail M9R Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Nissan X-Trail M9R Timing Chain Failure - Why 2.0 dCi Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Nissan X-Trail T31/T32 2.0 dCi Acenta, Tekna, N-Connecta (2007-2020, M9R)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain stretch, causing cam-crank correlation loss and P0011 fault codes.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace chain, tensioner, guides before failure
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early; 40k-60k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded tensioner, bench tested

problems.recommendation: If your X-Trail has rattling noise or P0011 codes below 90k miles, preventative chain replacement may save money. Above 100k miles or with loss of power, a rebuilt M9R with updated components offers better long-term value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt M9R engine prices for your Nissan X-Trail

problems.h4: Nissan X-Trail DPF Clogging - Why Diesel X-Trails Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Nissan X-Trail T31/T32 1.6 dCi, 2.0 dCi (2007-2020, R9M/M9R)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration; soot buildup triggers limp mode and power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-vehicle chemical cleaning or forced regeneration
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if filter undamaged; 20k-40k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: New or reconditioned DPF unit fitted
repairOptions.longevity: Required if filter cracked or melted; 60k+ mile life

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap addressing root cause
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if DPF failure caused secondary damage

problems.recommendation: For X-Trails used mainly on short trips, DPF cleaning plus driving habit changes may suffice. If cleaning fails repeatedly or turbo damage occurred, replacement with a rebuilt R9M/M9R avoids future DPF anxiety.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Nissan X-Trail diesel engine replacement

problems.group: Nissan X-Trail Timing Cover Oil Leak
problems.h4: Nissan X-Trail Timing Cover Oil Leak - The £1,000+ Repair Dilemma
problems.affectedModels: Nissan X-Trail T31 MR20DE, T32 MR20DD (2007-2020)
problems.rootCause: Timing cover gasket degradation allows oil seepage; labour-intensive access drives repair cost.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove ancillaries, replace cover gasket and seals
repairOptions.longevity: Effective fix; 50k+ mile life if done properly

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with updated seals
repairOptions.longevity: Better value if engine has other high-mileage wear

problems.recommendation: If your X-Trail petrol has only the oil leak and under 90k miles, seal replacement is cost-effective. For higher mileage or combined issues, a rebuilt MR20DE/MR20DD with warranty offers peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt MR20DE Nissan X-Trail engine prices

problems.group: Nissan X-Trail Turbo Oil Line Leak
problems.h4: Nissan X-Trail Turbo Oil Line Leak - Common Cause of Turbo Failure
problems.affectedModels: Nissan X-Trail T31/T32 1.6 dCi, 2.0 dCi (2007-2020, R9M/M9R)
problems.rootCause: Turbo feed/return line seals harden with heat cycles; oil starvation damages turbo bearings.

repairOptions.longevity: Preventative fix; effective if turbo undamaged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Reconditioned turbo fitted with new lines
repairOptions.longevity: Required if turbo bearings damaged; 40k-70k mile life

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap addressing root cause
repairOptions.longevity: Best if turbo failure caused metal contamination

problems.recommendation: Catch oil line leaks early with regular inspections. If turbo damage occurred or engine has high mileage, a rebuilt R9M/M9R with updated oil lines prevents repeat failures.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt Nissan X-Trail diesel engines with warranty

problems.h4: Nissan X-Trail Cam-Crank Correlation Code - The P0011 Warning Sign
problems.affectedModels: Nissan X-Trail T31/T32 M9R, R9M diesel (2007-2020)
problems.rootCause: Timing chain stretch alters cam-crank timing; ECU logs P0011 and may limit power to protect engine.

repairOptions.longevity: Only if codes were false; not a true fix for stretch
Effective if caught before valve contact; 50k+ mile life

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded components
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if chain failure caused valve/piston damage

problems.recommendation: P0011 on an X-Trail diesel is almost always timing chain stretch. If engine runs roughly or has rattling noise, replacement with a rebuilt unit avoids catastrophic failure risk.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Nissan X-Trail M9R engine replacement today

problems.group: Nissan X-Trail T30 YD22 Synchronization Failure
problems.h4: Nissan X-Trail T30 YD22 Synchronization Failure - No-Start After Timing Work
problems.affectedModels: Nissan X-Trail T30 2.2 dCi Sport, Columbia (2003-2007, YD22DDTi)
problems.rootCause: Incorrect cam-crank alignment during belt replacement causes synchronization loss and no-start condition.

repairOptions.longevity: Effective if no valve damage occurred; requires expertise

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with correct timing setup
repairOptions.longevity: Best if valves bent or owner wants warranty-backed solution

problems.recommendation: YD22 synchronization requires specialist equipment. If your T30 won't start after timing work, seek a Nissan specialist immediately. For high-mileage engines, rebuilt replacement offers better long-term security.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt YD22DDTi Nissan X-Trail engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Nissan X-Trail
finalCta.paragraph: From timing chain stretch on M9R diesels to DPF clogging on R9M units, Nissan X-Trail engine problems are well-documented but manageable with the right solution. EngineMarket connects you with trusted UK specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing the original failure point. Compare prices, warranty terms, and supply & fit options side-by-side-get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Nissan X-Trail Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Nissan X-Trail and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.intro: Every engine type is available through our UK network with typical price ranges based on your X-Trail variant, engine code and budget.

types.title: Nissan X-Trail Reconditioned Engines (Recon)

types.frontDisclaimer: Core components reused; warranty varies by supplier.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned X-Trail engines undergo strip, clean and inspection with replacement of worn bearings, seals and gaskets. Ideal for budget-conscious owners.
backBullets: Best for: X-Trail with documented service history

types.frontDescription: Comprehensive rebuild with upgraded components addressing known X-Trail failure points like timing chains.

engineSizes.h2: Nissan X-Trail Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Nissan X-Trail across UK generations is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Nissan X-Trail 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The smallest diesel option, the 1.5L K9K was fitted to entry-level X-Trail variants in select markets; shared with Qashqai, it offers economy but DPF sensitivity on urban use.
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan X-Trail 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan X-Trail 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: Introduced with the T32 generation, the 1.6L R9M replaced the 2.0L for improved economy; widely used but requires regular motorway runs to maintain DPF health.
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan X-Trail 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan X-Trail 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the X-Trail diesel range, the 2.0L M9R powered T31 and early T32 models; strong torque for 4WD use but timing chain stretch is a known high-mileage concern.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain stretch causing P0011 codes
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan X-Trail 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan X-Trail 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The early T30 diesel option, the 2.2L YD22DDTi offered robust torque but cam-crank synchronization sensitivity after timing belt work; now rare on UK roads.
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan X-Trail 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Nissan X-Trail 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: Late T32 facelift models received the Mercedes-sourced 1.3L HR13DDT turbo petrol; downsized efficiency with turbo complexity requiring careful maintenance.
items.cta: Compare Nissan X-Trail 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan X-Trail 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The most common petrol displacement across T30 and T31 generations; QR20DE and MR20DE units offer reliability but higher fuel consumption than diesel equivalents.
items.cta: Compare Nissan X-Trail 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan X-Trail 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The larger petrol option for T30/T31 4WD models; QR25DE provides stronger torque for towing but increased fuel consumption and oil usage at high mileage.
commonFailurePoints: Oil consumption from piston ring wear
items.cta: Compare Nissan X-Trail 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Nissan X-Trail 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The T33 e-POWER uses a 1.5L KR15DDT VC-Turbo as a generator for the electric drive system; variable compression technology for efficiency but hybrid complexity.
items.cta: Compare Nissan X-Trail 1.5L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.h2: Nissan X-Trail Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Nissan X-Trail engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from early QR20DE petrol to the latest KR15DDT e-POWER hybrid.

items.title: Nissan X-Trail Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel powered the majority of UK X-Trail sales across T31 and T32 generations, with M9R 2.0 dCi and R9M 1.6 dCi dominating replacement enquiries due to timing chain and DPF concerns.
items.descriptor: Diesel powered the majority of UK X-Trail sales across T31 and T32 generations, with M9R 2.0 dCi and R9M 1.6 dCi dominating replacement enquiries due to timing chain and DPF concerns.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan X-Trail Diesel Engine Families
families: M9R - 2.0 dCi workhorse (T31/T32), timing chain stretch at 80k-120k miles
R9M - 1.6 dCi downsized unit (T32), DPF sensitivity on urban use
YD22DDTi - early T30 2.2 dCi, cam-crank sync issues post-timing work
K9K - 1.5 dCi entry option (select markets), shared with Qashqai
knownFor: Strong torque and towing capability for 4WD SUV use
M9R timing chain stretch causing P0011 cam-crank correlation codes
DPF clogging on short-journey vehicles requiring specialist cleaning
High UK replacement demand on Acenta and Tekna diesel variants
items.cta: Get quotes for Nissan X-Trail Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Nissan X-Trail Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol X-Trail variants use QR, MR and HR engine families; generally more reliable than diesel but with higher fuel consumption and specific issues like timing cover leaks.
items.descriptor: Petrol X-Trail variants use QR, MR and HR engine families; generally more reliable than diesel but with higher fuel consumption and specific issues like timing cover leaks.
items.familiesLabel: Common Nissan X-Trail Petrol Engine Families
families: QR20DE/QR25DE - naturally aspirated T30/T31 units, reliable but thirsty
MR20DE/MR20DD - refined T31/T32 petrol, timing cover oil leak common
HR13DDT - Mercedes-sourced 1.3 turbo (late T32), turbo complexity
knownFor: Simpler maintenance than diesel, no DPF concerns
Timing cover oil leak requiring £1,000-£1,500 repair on MR engines
Higher fuel consumption impacting running costs for high-mileage owners
CVT transmission compatibility considerations on automatic variants

items.title: Nissan X-Trail Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: T32 hybrid and T33 e-POWER variants use petrol engines as generators for electric drive; complex but efficient, with specialist replacement considerations.
items.descriptor: T32 hybrid and T33 e-POWER variants use petrol engines as generators for electric drive; complex but efficient, with specialist replacement considerations.
families: MR20DD - 2.0 direct injection petrol generator (T32 hybrid)
Petrol engine generates electricity; electric motors drive the wheels
No traditional gearbox; seamless acceleration and regenerative braking
Battery health inspection recommended during any engine replacement
importantNotes: e-POWER is series hybrid: engine never directly drives wheels
Replacement requires hybrid system specialist; not all garages equipped
Availability more limited than standard engines; allow extra lead time

engineYears.h2: Nissan X-Trail Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Nissan X-Trail's UK production history, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: T30 Launch: QR Petrol & Early Diesel
years.description: The original X-Trail launched with simple, reliable petrol engines and the robust YD22 diesel.
keyChanges: First-generation T30 platform introduced with car-like SUV styling
QR20DE 2.0L petrol and QR25DE 2.5L petrol offered with manual or auto
YD22DDTi 2.2L diesel added mid-cycle for improved economy
knownFor: Simple, robust engineering with easy DIY maintenance access
YD22 cam-crank sync sensitivity after timing belt replacement
Higher fuel consumption on petrol variants vs later diesels
Strong off-road capability with ALL MODE 4x4-i system

years.preview: T30 Facelift: Refined Diesel & Petrol Options
years.description: Mid-life updates brought improved emissions compliance and trim diversification.
keyChanges: Facelift styling with revised grille and interior updates
YD22DDTi diesel received emissions improvements for Euro 4
Columbia and Sport trims expanded the range
knownFor: Proven reliability with low-cost servicing for petrol variants
YD22 synchronization issues remain a post-repair risk
Growing used market demand for affordable 4WD SUV
Timing belt replacement critical at 60k mile intervals

M9R 2.0 dCi diesel replaced YD22 as primary powertrain
MR20DE petrol introduced alongside carry-over QR25DE
knownFor: M9R timing chain stretch emerges as high-mileage concern (80k-120k miles)
Improved interior quality and on-road refinement vs T30
Strong towing capability with 2.0 dCi torque
Early examples show timing cover oil leaks on MR20DE

M9R received minor calibration updates for emissions
CVT automatic option expanded on petrol variants

MR20DE timing cover oil leak repair cost £1,000-£1,500

faq.h2: Nissan X-Trail Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Nissan X-Trail engine in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Nissan X-Trail engines typically cost £1,400-£4,500 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Diesel M9R/R9M units sit at £1,800-£3,200, while petrol QR/MR engines range £1,400-£2,800. Supply & fit adds £400-£900 labour.
keyPoints: M9R 2.0 dCi rebuilt: £1,800-£3,200 supply only
R9M 1.6 dCi rebuilt: £1,900-£3,200 supply only
QR20DE/MR20DE petrol: £1,400-£2,600 supply only
Labour for supply & fit typically £400-£900 extra
All EngineMarket suppliers include 12-month warranty minimum
items.cta: Compare live Nissan X-Trail engine replacement prices now

items.question: What is the most common engine problem on Nissan X-Trail diesel models?
items.answer: Timing chain stretch on M9R and R9R diesel engines is the most reported failure, causing cam-crank correlation codes (P0011), rattling noise, and loss of power. DPF clogging is a close second on urban-driven vehicles.
keyPoints: M9R timing chain stretch typically appears 80k-120k miles
P0011 fault code indicates cam-crank timing loss
DPF clogging causes limp mode on short-journey diesel X-Trails
Turbo oil line leaks are another frequent diesel failure point
Rebuilt engines include upgraded timing components to prevent repeat
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt M9R Nissan X-Trail engines

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Nissan X-Trail or should I sell it?
items.answer: If your X-Trail is T31/T32 with under 120k miles and good bodywork, a rebuilt engine at £1,800-£3,200 often makes financial sense versus part-exchange value of £3k-£6k. For T30 or high-mileage examples, selling may be preferable.
keyPoints: T31/T32 X-Trails retain £3k-£6k part-ex value in good condition
Rebuilt M9R/R9M engine costs £1,800-£3,200 supply only
Supply & fit labour adds £400-£900 typically
12-month warranty on rebuilt units protects your investment
T30 models often have lower residual value; selling may be better
items.cta: Compare Nissan X-Trail engine replacement vs part-exchange value

items.question: Which Nissan X-Trail engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: Petrol QR20DE and QR25DE engines in T30/T31 models are generally considered the most reliable, with fewer complex components than diesel. However, they consume more fuel, making them better suited to lower-mileage owners.
keyPoints: QR20DE/QR25DE petrol: simple, robust, fewer failure points
M9R/R9M diesel: strong torque but timing chain/DPF concerns
MR20DE petrol: refined but timing cover oil leak common
HR13DDT turbo petrol: efficient but turbo complexity adds risk
KR15DDT e-POWER: new technology; long-term reliability still proving
items.cta: Compare reliable petrol engine options for your Nissan X-Trail

items.question: How long should a Nissan X-Trail engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: With full service history and timely maintenance, Nissan X-Trail engines can reach 150,000-200,000 miles. Diesel M9R units often require timing chain attention at 80k-120k miles to achieve this longevity.
keyPoints: Full service history critical for reaching 150k+ miles
M9R timing chain inspection recommended at 80k miles
DPF regeneration awareness essential for diesel longevity
Oil changes every 10k miles or 12 months maximum
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components can match new-engine life
items.cta: Find Nissan X-Trail engines with 12-month warranty coverage

items.question: Can I replace my Nissan X-Trail diesel engine with a petrol one?
items.answer: Engine swaps between diesel and petrol on the X-Trail are technically complex and rarely cost-effective due to ECU, wiring, exhaust and mounting differences. Sticking with the original fuel type is strongly recommended.
keyPoints: Diesel-to-petrol swap requires ECU, wiring, exhaust changes
Mounting points and transmission compatibility differ
MOT and insurance implications for non-standard conversions
Cost of conversion often exceeds price of correct rebuilt engine
EngineMarket specialists supply correct-spec replacements only
items.cta: Get quotes for correct-spec Nissan X-Trail engine replacement

items.question: What causes the P0011 fault code on my Nissan X-Trail 2.0 dCi?
items.answer: P0011 on X-Trail M9R diesel almost always indicates timing chain stretch causing cam-crank correlation loss. Ignoring it risks valve-piston contact and catastrophic engine damage.
keyPoints: P0011 = camshaft position timing over-advanced or system performance
Caused by M9R timing chain stretch at 80k-120k miles
Symptoms: rattling noise, rough idle, loss of power, EML on
Preventative chain replacement costs £800-£1,200 specialist
Rebuilt M9R with upgraded tensioner prevents repeat failure
items.cta: Compare rebuilt M9R engines for P0011 Nissan X-Trail

items.question: How much does DPF cleaning cost on a Nissan X-Trail diesel?
items.answer: Professional DPF cleaning for Nissan X-Trail diesel models costs £250-£450 at specialists. If the filter is damaged or melted, replacement costs £700-£1,400 supply only.
keyPoints: Off-vehicle chemical cleaning: £250-£450 typical
Forced regeneration attempt: £150-£300 if successful
New DPF unit: £700-£1,400 supply only
Prevention: regular motorway runs to aid regeneration
Repeated DPF failures may indicate underlying turbo/EGR issues
items.cta: Find Nissan X-Trail diesel engine specialists near you

items.question: Are rebuilt Nissan X-Trail engines as good as new?
items.answer: Quality rebuilt X-Trail engines from EngineMarket suppliers use upgraded components addressing known failure points (e.g., timing chain tensioners), often exceeding original specification, with 12-month unlimited mileage warranty.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines receive new pistons, rings, bearings, seals
Upgraded timing components prevent repeat M9R/R9M failures
Bench-tested before dispatch for reliability assurance
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard across network
Cost typically 40-60% less than main dealer new engine
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs new Nissan X-Trail engine costs

items.question: How quickly can I get a replacement engine for my Nissan X-Trail?
items.answer: Used Nissan X-Trail engines are often available within 24-48 hours. Rebuilt units typically take 3-7 working days depending on engine code and supplier workload. Supply & fit service adds 1-2 days for installation.
keyPoints: Used engines: often available within 24-48 hours
Rebuilt engines: 3-7 working days typical lead time
Supply & fit installation: 1-2 days at specialist garage
Nationwide delivery available across UK network
Enter registration above for live availability and quotes
